get.description: Return dashboard status rows for source documents.

post.description: Synchronize extraction/ranking statuses for all documents.

get.description: Return image prompts for a specific scene.
List prompts with optional filters, across all books or a specific book.
List prompts across a book with optional filters and pagination.
Fetch a single image prompt by identifier.

post.description: Generate multiple metadata variants for an image prompt without persisting them.

patch.description: Update stored metadata for an image prompt.

get.description: Return a paginated list of scene extractions with optional filters.
Expose the available filter options for scene extractions.
Fetch a single scene extraction by its identifier.

post.description:
Generate images for a specific scene.

Creates a tracked pipeline run that generates fresh prompts and images
for the given scene. The run executes in the background.

get.description: Return the highest ranked scenes, optionally filtered by book.
Return ranking history for a specific scene.
Fetch a single scene ranking by identifier.
Return list of distinct providers used in generated images.
List generated images with optional filters and pagination.

Fetch a single generated image by identifier with full context.
Returns image metadata along with prompt text and raw scene text.

patch.description: Update the approval status of a generated image.

get.description: Stream the binary image file for a generated image.
Return generated images for a specific scene extraction.
Return generated images for a specific image prompt.

post.description: Trigger subtle remix prompt generation for a specific generated image.
Trigger a custom remix using user-supplied prompt text for a generated image.

Trigger image generation for a selection of prompts.

This endpoint allows you to generate images based on various filters:
- By book_slug (optionally with chapter_range)
- By specific scene_ids
- By specific prompt_ids

In dry-run mode, it will return a count of 0 and no IDs.
Otherwise, it returns the list of generated image IDs.

Queue an approved image for posting to configured social media services.

The image must have user_approved=True. If the cooldown period has passed
since the last post, the image will be posted immediately. Otherwise, it
will be added to the queue for later posting.

get.description:
Get the social media posting status for an image.

Returns all posting records for the image, along with summary flags
indicating whether it has been posted or is currently queued.

post.description:
Requeue all failed social media posts so they will be retried.

Optionally filter by service name. Requeued posts will be picked up by
the scheduler on its next run.

put.description:
Replace a generated image with a cropped version.

Accepts a multipart file upload containing the cropped image data
and overwrites the original file on disk.

get.description: Return global defaults and active art-style catalog entries.

patch.description: Update global defaults for scenes-per-run and prompt art style.

post.description: Probe configured providers so users can validate setup before a run.

get.description: List active art styles available to users.
Return recommended/other style pools as line-oriented settings arrays.

put.description: Replace active recommended/other style pools from full list payloads.

post.description: Reset recommended/other style pools to the hardcoded defaults.
Create a pipeline run and execute it in the background.

get.description: Return the current state of a pipeline run.

ApiErrorDetail.title: ApiErrorDetail
ApiErrorDetail.description: Structured app error payload used for migrated non-validation responses.

ApiErrorResponse.title: ApiErrorResponse
ApiErrorResponse.description:
Canonical app error response envelope.

FastAPI validation errors still use the framework's default `detail` array shape.
Untouched legacy routes may continue to return string `detail` values during rollout.
